row_range.note: Three-block data structure. Sequential block contains Dec 16 2025-Mar 18 2026 Eli/Sent messages; after ROW-8595 the sequential block contains Nov-Dec 2024 data appearing out of chronological order (ROW-8679 Dec 14 2024 assigned to B-020; ROW-8877 Jan 5 2025 assigned to B-021 as peripheral). Parallel block covers full Dec 16 2025-Apr 7 2026 range.

batch_metadata.note: Phase C. Dec 2025-Apr 2026. Three-block data structure with out-of-order sequential block data. Contains S-010: ROW-15082 response-time blame attribution (Feb 11 2026). Dataset closing period.

flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-15077 (Received, Feb 11): 'I'll take your no response as a no' | ROW-15079 (Received, Feb 11): 'Lame excuse' | ROW-15082 (Received, Feb 11): 'Your idea that you don't need to answer me until you feel like it causes these issues'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: Feb 11 pickup coordination dispute. Context: Eli was sick (post-flu/pneumonia), asked if he could pick up Olivia if he felt well enough. He didn't respond promptly due to work meetings. Melissa escalates: ROW-15077 'I'll take your no response as a no' — unilateral scheduling assumption without confirmation (parallel to S-005 ROW-14188 mechanism). ROW-15079 'Lame excuse' — contemptuous dismissal of Eli's health/work explanation. ROW-15082 'Your idea that you don't need to answer me until you feel like it causes these issues' — response-time blame attribution. Mechanism: Melissa defines Eli's response timeline as the cause of scheduling problems, establishing a norm that Eli must respond on-demand or be held responsible for consequences. Per ADD-004: response-demand framing confirmed; Sev-1 assigned (single episode, non-parenting-time context). The 'I'll take your no response as a no' mechanism is a unilateral-assumption pressure tool — forcing Eli to respond or lose parenting opportunity. ARC-RF07-001-S010.
verbatim_key_messages: ROW-15077 (Received, Feb 11): 'I'll take your no response as a no'
ROW-15082 (Received, Feb 11): 'Your idea that you don't need to answer me until you feel like it causes these issues'
flagged_messages.calibration_notes: Sev-1: response-time blame attribution in a pickup-coordination context. Context note: Eli was ill — Melissa's 'lame excuse' dismissal of a health explanation while demanding on-demand response is the RF-07 component. ADD-004 response-demand framing confirmed.

arc_state_snapshot.note: ARC-RF07-001 ACTIVE. S-010 added (ROW-15082, response-time blame, Sev-1). Arc now at 10 signals. Dataset closing period — B-031 is the final batch. Out-of-order sequential block data (ROW-8679, ROW-8877) assigned to their chronological batches (B-020 and B-021 respectively) with data structure notes.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: Phase C — Dec 2025-Apr 2026. Variable conflict density. Feb 11 pickup dispute notable. Apr 7 dataset approaches closing. RF-04 continued: Feb 15 admitted-incident amplification; Mar 28 explicit alcoholism accusation; Apr 7 pre-closing contempt.
batch_observations.rf07_assessment: One primary signal (Sev-1 response-time blame). S-010 reinforces the on-demand-response-norm sub-pattern: Melissa defines when Eli must respond and frames his failure to meet her timeline as causing scheduling problems. The 'I'll take your no response as a no' mechanism is a recurring pressure tool (parallel to S-005 Aug 21 'I'll assume you are not picking her up'). Phase C shows the pattern maintained without attenuation.
batch_observations.custodial_context: Feb 11 is a pickup coordination scenario — Eli's parenting time is the context (Eli asking if he can pick up Olivia). Melissa's deployment of response-time blame during a parenting-time request elevates ADD-004 consideration but the primary mechanism is response-time norm-setting rather than custodial intrusion.
